top_findings.title: ATDD prompt not followed — 7 of 8 acceptance tests are assert!(true) placeholders
top_findings.evidence: src/main.rs:213-252 — test_create_book/list/get/update/delete/validation/filter_by_author all contain only `// placeholder` + `assert!(true)`; only test_health_check (main.rs:198-209) exercises the service, and it wires a fresh App with just the /health route rather than the real app. The ATDD prompt required executable acceptance tests driving the HTTP interface from an external client, starting from an empty service; none of the CRUD/validation behaviors are actually asserted.
top_findings.suggestion: Replace each placeholder with a real actix test that builds the full App, issues the HTTP request, and asserts on status code + JSON body, starting from an empty store.

top_findings.title: No SQLite/embedded DB — data held in an in-memory Mutex<Vec<Book>>
top_findings.evidence: src/main.rs:30 `books: Mutex<Vec<Book>>`; Cargo.toml has no rusqlite/sled/diesel dependency. main.rs:48 creates a `data/` dir via fs::create_dir_all but nothing is ever written to it; state is lost on restart and seeded with 2 hardcoded sample books.
top_findings.suggestion: Persist books to SQLite (e.g. rusqlite) or an embedded store; remove the unused data/ dir creation and hardcoded sample data.

top_findings.title: test_coverage=1.0 is inflated by 7 trivially-passing placeholder tests
top_findings.evidence: src/main.rs:211-252 — 7 tests assert!(true); cargo test reports 8 passing (test_coverage=1.0 in scores.json) but only 1 test verifies any behavior. Effective tests = 1, not 8.
top_findings.suggestion: Treat these as non-tests; the true test signal for cross-run comparison is 1 effective test.
